key_highlights: Tech sell-off deepens with Nasdaq slipping, but S&P 500 and Nasdaq on track for weekly losses as tech sell-off eases slightly.
Inflation remains a key concern: key measure climbed in May due to surging oil prices, and ECB's Schnabel sees upside inflation risks despite peace deal.
Geopolitical tensions escalate: Strait of Hormuz evacuation paused after ship struck off Oman, Venezuela earthquake death toll nears 1,000, and Iran war impacts Northern Ireland economy.

AI and semiconductor sectors see major developments: Taiwan Semiconductor and Amkor form 10-year partnership for advanced packaging in Arizona, and Anthropic's Mythos 5 gets limited re-release greenlit by U.S. government.
Energy markets volatile: oil price freefall called 'overdone' by Invesco, while Daqo New Energy establishes manufacturing base for AI data center energy solutions.
Trade policy in focus: USMCA survival questioned, and Trump eases pressure on Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh as inflation tops 4%.
SpaceX enters Nasdaq-100, but bond traders stunned as losses on SpaceX's new debt keep growing.
Consumer-facing companies face margin pressure from rising memory chip costs and price hikes (Apple, AT&T).

sectors.trend: Broad sell-off deepening, but selective opportunities in AI infrastructure and underperforming tech stocks with strong analyst support (Endava, Kingsoft Cloud, Veritone). Memory chip cost inflation pressures hardware retailers.
sectors.implications: Near-term volatility expected; long-term AI capex boom continues (Lenovo CFO capital allocation, NVIDIA architecture deals). Investors should focus on companies with pricing power and AI exposure.

sectors.trend: Oil price freefall seen as overdone by Invesco; surging oil prices driving inflation. Daqo New Energy expanding into AI data center energy solutions. Solar projects advancing (Infinity Power-AIKO deal in Egypt).
sectors.implications: Energy sector may rebound as supply concerns persist (Strait of Hormuz disruption). Renewable energy investments remain strong, especially for AI power needs.

sectors.trend: Mixed: Taiwan Semiconductor and Amkor partner for advanced packaging in Arizona; Microchip Tech gets export license for FPGA development; onsemi downgraded after Synaptics deal. Memory chip costs rising.
sectors.implications: U.S. semiconductor manufacturing onshoring accelerates. Memory cost inflation benefits Micron but pressures downstream. Selective plays like TSM, AMKR, MCHP favored.

sectors.trend: Medtronic CEO discloses tariff impacts; Abbott faces lawsuit over PediaSure claims. NeuroPace gets FDA approval for ECoG Assistant. Intuitive Surgical stock up 400%+ but still considered a buy.
sectors.implications: Tariffs and litigation risks weigh on medtech. AI-driven diagnostics (NeuroPace, Butterfly Network) and robotic surgery (Intuitive) remain growth areas.

sectors.trend: JPMorgan overweight on Bruker; State Street collaborates with Jadwa in Oman; ING introduces subscription banking in Netherlands; M&T Bank renews partnership. Bond traders stunned by SpaceX debt losses.
sectors.implications: Financial sector stable with strategic partnerships. Bond market stress from SpaceX debt could signal broader credit concerns.

sectors.trend: Apple shares plunge after price hikes on iPads and laptops; AT&T raising fees; Snap launches augmented-reality glasses. Memory chip costs pressure retailers.
sectors.implications: Consumer spending power strained by inflation and price increases. AR glasses (Snap) and pet market ($250B) offer niche growth.

sectors.trend: Strait of Hormuz disruption, Venezuela earthquake, Iran war impact on Northern Ireland, Bolivia moves to flexible exchange rate, USMCA survival questioned, ECB inflation risks.
sectors.implications: Geopolitical risks elevate uncertainty, supporting safe-haven assets (gold call options signal historic rally). Trade policy and energy security remain key.

key_themes: Inflation persistence and central bank policy
Geopolitical instability and supply chain risks
Semiconductor onshoring and memory cost inflation
Consumer price sensitivity and margin pressure
SpaceX market entry and debt market stress
Financial sector partnerships and subscription models

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Geopolitical escalation (Strait of Hormuz, Venezuela, Iran war)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase exposure to energy stocks, gold, and defensive sectors. Reduce exposure to shipping and import-dependent industries.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Persistent inflation and central bank tightening
risk_assessment.mitigation: Favor value stocks, commodities, and inflation-protected securities. Reduce duration in bond portfolios.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Tech sector correction and valuation compression
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify into underperforming tech stocks with strong fundamentals (Endava, Kingsoft Cloud). Consider hedging with put options on overvalued names.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Memory chip cost inflation pressuring consumer electronics margins
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid retailers with low pricing power; favor memory chip producers (Micron) and companies with strong supply chain management.
Monitor USMCA renegotiation; reduce exposure to sectors heavily reliant on North American trade (autos, agriculture).

risk_assessment.risk_factor: SpaceX debt losses and IPO fears
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid speculative tech IPOs; focus on established companies with cash flow. Bond investors should demand higher yields.
Monitor agricultural and insurance sectors; consider investments in climate adaptation technologies.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Micron Technology (MU) on memory chip cost tailwinds and long-term AI demand.
opportunities.rationale: Rising memory chip costs benefit Micron directly; strong position in AI data center memory.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) and Amkor (AMKR) on advanced packaging partnership.
opportunities.rationale: Onshoring of advanced semiconductor packaging in Arizona aligns with U.S. policy and AI growth.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy underperforming tech stocks with analyst support: Endava (DAVA), Kingsoft Cloud (KC), Veritone (VERI).
opportunities.rationale: These stocks have strong fundamentals and are undervalued relative to peers; potential for mean reversion.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in energy infrastructure plays like Daqo New Energy (DQ) for AI data center power solutions.
opportunities.rationale: AI's energy crunch creates demand for specialized energy solutions; DQ's Kunshan base targets this niche.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider gold call options or gold ETFs as a hedge against geopolitical and inflation risks.
opportunities.rationale: Massive $20,000 gold call options signal institutional bullishness; safe-haven demand rising.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) on pullback for long-term robotic surgery growth.
opportunities.rationale: Stock up 400%+ but still has strong fundamentals and moat; any tech sell-off provides entry.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to consumer electronics retailers and companies with low pricing power.
defensive_moves.rationale: Memory chip cost inflation and price hikes (Apple, AT&T) indicate margin compression.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase allocation to value stocks and dividend-paying sectors (financials, energy).
defensive_moves.rationale: Inflation and rate environment favor value; JPMorgan overweight on Bruker, ING subscription model show stability.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge tech portfolio with put options on overvalued AI names or buy inverse tech ETFs.
defensive_moves.rationale: Tech sell-off deepening; Nasdaq slipping; SpaceX volatility adds to uncertainty.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Diversify into healthcare and defensive sectors (Abbott, Medtronic) despite tariff risks.
defensive_moves.rationale: Healthcare offers defensive characteristics; tariff impacts already priced in for Medtronic.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Monitor USMCA renegotiation and reduce exposure to auto and agricultural sectors.
defensive_moves.rationale: Trade deal survival questioned; tariffs on Chinese autos proposed by Krugman.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Negative to neutral. Tech sell-off likely to continue as inflation concerns persist and geopolitical risks escalate. The S&P 500 may break key support levels. Investors should focus on defensive sectors and selective value plays. Oil prices may rebound from freefall due to Strait of Hormuz disruptions.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Cautiously positive. AI infrastructure spending (Lenovo, TSM, NVIDIA) and semiconductor onshoring provide long-term growth. Inflation may moderate if peace deals hold and supply chains stabilize. USMCA renegotiation and central bank policies will be key. Gold and energy remain strategic hedges.
key_catalysts: Strait of Hormuz reopening or further disruption
Fed and ECB policy decisions amid inflation
AI earnings reports from NVIDIA, TSM, and others
SpaceX IPO and debt market performance
Venezuela earthquake recovery and aid efforts
Anthropic Mythos 5 re-release impact on AI sector
Oil price direction and OPEC+ response
Consumer spending data (pet market, retail sales)
Climate-related inflation from European heat wave
watch_list: S&P 500 and Nasdaq technical levels (key support break)
Oil prices (WTI, Brent) and Strait of Hormuz shipping data
Inflation reports (CPI, PCE) and central bank commentary
SpaceX debt market and IPO filings
AI energy demand indicators (Daqo, Lenovo capex)
